raw_consumer_statement:
American Express is attempting to collect an alleged balance of {$160.00}, but they : Closed my account Blocked my ability to log in Refuse to provide a bill or itemized statement Refuse to explain the charges Continue calling me and threatening collections Send only automated template letters instead of answering Ignored multiple certified written requests for a billing statement I have repeatedly asked American Express to provide a bill so I can see what the charge is for and pay it. They have refused to send one.

I can not view my account online because it is disabled, so I can not verify or pay anything. They continue to threaten collections without ever providing a statement. 

I am not refusing to pay. American Express is preventing me from paying by denying account access and refusing to provide the bill. 


What Happened My account was closed without explanation. 

A balance appeared. 

I requested billing documentation multiple times by certified mail. 

American Express refused to provide a statement. 

They will not allow me to log in to view or pay the balance. 

They continue threatening collections anyway. 

Harm I have been : Unable to verify the alleged balance Unable to pay due to blocked access and no bill Stressed by repeated phone calls and threats Spending time sending certified letters asking for a basic statement Resolution Requested I am asking the CFPB to require American Express to : Provide a full billing statement for the alleged balance. 

Restore access to the account OR provide a written payment method.

Stop collection threats until they provide the bill. 

Provide a real written response, not automated template letters. 

I am simply asking for the opportunity to see the charges and pay them. American Express is refusing to give me the basic information required to do so.
